channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1 — 156 replies — 569.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1

Open thread • Replies: 156 • Updated: 2026-06-24 23:21 UTC

OP

Brothers,

SpaceX has begun to rise. Its success or fall is of little importance to us in the short term.

In the meanwhile we must devise ways to gain a spot at the table with our kin in the six figure club.

I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.

However, I would be remiss to not tell the truth that I haven't a clue what we should study.

So instead I suggest we create an eggregore for these threads. Others made monero-chan, bobo and sminem

I am thinking we create one based on a girl who would be willing to believe in us while we are poor and brave the pain throughout having to witness our more disappointing moments of action. Never losing faith in us, she is always there. Yes. This is how we shall spend our time.

At any rate, that is my suggestion for tonight's fun. I have to work in the morning so let's not get too rowdy. Take it or leave it. If you will take it, then be sure to double it and pass it on to the next anon.

Let us commence the general!

Come one, come all!

Early replies (first 3)

62373772
>>62373752
Jannies will surely delete these threads. Instead of creating an eggregore why not try something new and usurp an old one. It may be a derivative of one which has already been made.

62373798
>>62373772
Hello anon thanks for joining. You are poster #1. Your anime image shall be the eggregore. The "coffee" in the image is rather a good omen. We all know images with hot girls accompanied by a brief post on coffee attracts all the attention.

Now that matter is settled, help me add some links to this post here. This will be the header for the start of each general

Helpful links

>Educational materials
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Looking to try the TCG route?
https://youtube.com/@alphainvestments69?si=tDAPjnRSZhyCIGP5

>Lost all hope?
https://www.indeed.com/


>Hidden in plain sight
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Limitless_(film)

>Games to strengthen you financial acumen
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Learn your from your betters
https://ia600609.us.archive.org/3/items/the-house-of-rothschild-ferguson-niall/The%20House%20of%20Rothschild%20-%20Ferguson%2C%20Niall.pdf
What is the name of that character in your post?

62373799
>>62373772
verily, a true fact

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62398809
>>62398799
How much are you putting down? I'm thinking of doing $1000 all in picks and making a folder that way

62398813
There, lets read over these three

62398868
>>62398799
You are better off getting a job

62398876
>>62398868
https://voca.ro/1aI6fGYlNRNN

62398905
>>62394942
Don’t get that either. Even if you only bought full shares how could you not afford 200 dollars worth of shares? Is this under 10k net worth or under 100$ net worth

62398985
>>62376699
> you're arguing with a script and losing

62399017
>>62380691
bump

62399272
>>62398789
>Wavelength (λ) (THE SYMBOL IN HALF LIFE)

Distance between repeating points of a wave (e.g., crest to crest). Determines frequency and, for light, color.

>Amplitude:

Maximum height of the wave. Determines intensity (brightness for light, loudness for sound).

>Phase

Position of a wave within its cycle. Determines how waves add or cancel when combined.

>Polarization

Direction of oscillation of a transverse wave. For light, it is the orientation of the electric field.

62399358
Tomorrow we shall give predictive programming a try as photonics will likely be a difficult venture

62399394
10k- networth reporting in! You guys are too smart for me with all those grand ideas.

62400367
>>62398789
tl;dr

62401110
7 figure club. I already lost more than 10k today at market open. Stay poor fags

62401765
I just wrote a super long post with all sorts of trade basics and an emphasis for this group to define normal position exit conditions so you can tangibly ask anons "did you exit before, during, or after the standardized 4chan-exit signals?" Along with recommending covered calls for slow moving but trend holding markets like gold/BTc/xmr or oil when watching forex markets. 10y+dxy are good to watch too.
Blah blah blah
But this whole thread degenerated into trying to sell "art", and not every anon working together for every group success to contribute to all that participate, but rather instead for every anon to work on their own project, by themselves, but as a group selling the same products/watering down their labor's value.

>>62401110
This is who you should ask for advice. This anon only lost 1% of his shit n that's if he's only got 1m. Not a big deal. Don't you anons want to be able to lose 10k casually n be unbothered yet responsible?

62401809
>>62401765
I did it the hard way. Degree in finance. Work in finance. Almost 20 years. Been investing since the 08 crash. There's no quick trick or easy money. Crypto is bullshit. Just keep buying.

62401893
Six figures is truly hell, I miss being proud of 70k.

62403333
>>62401765
It was one art guy

62403337
>>62401765
This general also is a work in progress, I am hoping 10 generals from now we have a much better work flow, plan and ultimately success. In some capacity.

62404260
Bump

62404574
No point in this general. Fastest way to make 10k is just to work for a bit, you shouldn’t be investing

62404709
Bump

62404737
>>62373752
its a spacex thread?
its time to buy o better buy in few days one week

62405715
bump

62407354
Page 8 bump, /biz/ must be losing money again because there's an uptick in activity.

62407450
almost 9 months since i started my first job after college
this feels stagnant and joyless
what exactly do i need to do to escape wageslavery and poverty

62407931
Someone send me ten grand so I can join the club, thanks

62408558
>>62407450
What were you doing in 2017, 2021 and December of 2024?

62408565
>>62407354
Thank you, my wage job prevents me from dedicating my energies to this thread but I should have some time soon

62408584
>54 PBITD
So you're the dumb nigger who ruined this board eh?
Gonna claim everyones the same person and mass report threads/posts again lol
JFC this boards dead get off 4chan while you still can anyone left are pajeet scammers.

62408588
>>62408565
>>62407354
See he's a reddit pajeet scammer who samefags on a dead board and claims everyone is a liar lol.

62408611
>>62408584
Wtf are you on about dude, yes I made 54 posts over the span of 8 days. I'm surprised you're not taking your own advice and leaving 4chan.

item.title: >boss put me on a PIP 2 months ago >been trying to find jobs a few months before that cuz I realized there was no pleasi… — 139 replies — 556.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>boss put me on a PIP 2 months ago >been trying to find jobs a few months before that cuz I realized there was no pleasi…

Open thread • Replies: 139 • Updated: 2026-06-24 23:37 UTC

OP

>boss put me on a PIP 2 months ago
>been trying to find jobs a few months before that cuz I realized there was no pleasing him
>haven't found any jobs, its unreal how bad the market is, have this bad feeling its never going to get better
>PIP ends on Tuesday
I am an engineer. I started off as a tech and worked my ass off getting bachelors full time to escape manual labor. Now im gonna be that goy with a masters degree who took a 500 percent pay cut to do repetitive ligament destroying work. I honestly feel like god did this beacuse its too much bad things happening at once that are culminating into my total demise.

Early replies (first 3)

62388691
>>62388676
>500 percent pay cut to do repetitive ligament destroying work
Trades pay more than ever, especially vs pajeet-saturated office work. Skill issue

62388694
>>62388676
My entire life has been nothing but fighting tooth and nail, working myself 1000x harder than anyone anywhere ever for 0.001% of what anyone else gets for free. I have felt that god is purposely shitting on me pretty much my whole life. It doesn't get better, it only ever gets worse. If anything happens to me that seem to be a good thing, I have 6 months max to enjoy it because that'll be taken from me and then I'll be beaten up and crushed down for added effect. It's a real comedy out there.

So don't worry bro, you have a lot of time ahead of you to suffer far more. If there really is a god, he must be the most perfectly evil of entities.

62388697
The worst part is that in my meeting with my boss today he didnt mention if I will pass the PIP. He said he needed to talk with some people including HR. So 90 percent chance he is going to fire me but just enough chance he wont that I have to spend the weekend waiting instead of processing the pain. And if he doesnt fire me, I will be a totally buckbroken employee who will never care about a rasie and work 60 hours weeks. Like a absurd housewife that has no were else to go and she tries to esapce and gets rapped(applies to ghost jobs) even worse so she comes back to him. Thats me. I used to think my relationship with work was like between a man and a woman, were I am the man that picks and chooses women and is the one who leaves but its all turned around past couple of years. I am nothing but a side pieace female whore to these companies now, they treat me like a contract worker without paying me like one. They got us with not giving raises to match inflation, they got us with return to work mandates and now, they are going to get us by firing us and leaving us with nothing. U will get put on a PIP too, at the worst time possible also. They wont even lay u off anymore, they want to save a little bit of money by denying us unemployment insurance by firing us through a PIP.


Was being in ur 30s a good excperince back in the day? There is no way human civilization would have gotten to were it is if it was always this bad for everyone. Of course, I am a faggot and just suck but the fsct of the matter is that PIPs and no jobs has been a very common trope on the internet lately

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62395717
>>62395641
Not every corporation runs the same
Shocking

62395927
My dream is, if my current gig works out (which will leave me with a lot of cash), is to start a software shop where the work-day is capped at 4 days. I don't care about making it big, I just want to spend my time at work with other people who are happy and live decent enough lives. Since it's an unexploited niche I wager I can gather some pretty decent talent.

Fuck this race to the bottom, I don't wanna be rich I wanna be happy

62395938
>>62395717
>>62395641

It's actually more about your boss. 90% of bosses don't gaf as long as you generally do what they say. There is a 10% of proactive dicks who are absolutely looking to roll heads.

62398374
>>62395927
Just give them wfh and treat them like human beings, engineers dont mind working overtime if its for the right person.


Also ye i had similar dreams to start a plesant working place even at the expense of profits but I mostly want to do it out of spite at this point

62399956
>>62388711
>ah yes did i tell you? all women around me have fancy jobs with big salaries and 100% remote positions and company trips all over the world
jewish daycare

62399988
>>62388694
I hold LINK too, brother

62400021
>>62388676
I was in a similar position like you OP. I disagreed with the staff engineer, he bad mouthed me to manager and I landed on a PIP. I spent 6 months on a PIP, looking for a job (I'm in Germany, they can't really fire me).
Mental was the hardest part. This is a process designed to break you down. What really helped me was to disengage with the work process, say nothing, do the bare minimum, wear my poker face.
After 6 months, I found a new job, negotiated an exit with a severance package.
I am not gonna lie, this was a shitty experience, but I learned a lot from it.

I wish you best of luck OP. Don't let them wear you down.

62401552
>>62388676
AI training is a decent stopgap while you're looking for work. Mercor and Micro1 are best, Handshake's okay, Outlier sucks but doesn't pull shenanigans with your pay (I've heard that Alignerr does).

62402291
>>62400021
Dude im at like 8 months in total i just a took a month long break at the baggining of the year. I would have found a job by now.. I sidnt even disagree with anybody, they are just bullying me and breaking my spirit. Idk why they haven't fired me ye.t today is the last day of my PIP and I have heard nothing. I really just wanna ask my boss if they are gonna can me or not

62404970
>>62402291
Well, what happened?

62405224
>>62388694
>working myself 1000x harder
No, let's bring you back to reality. You're on /biz/ and the type of hard work you stated is reading white papers and then falling for the next make-it-big scam. You should have chased an honest life, but you're gambling and that does not pay.

62405704
>>62393491
Unemployment usually pays out. The only time it doesn’t is if you get fired for not showing up.

62406146
>>62404970
PIP ended yesterday and nothing so far. I won't be out of the clear until week after next, they could just be finalizing paperwork. If they dont fire me it will be hilarious beacuse they have been acting exactly like they would

62406163
>>62405704
That makes sence cuz the only time i was denied was when I was too sick to show up for work for 2 weeks

62407227
>>62399956
WebMs don't have sound on this board, so here's the original: https://x.com/LindyTasteful/status/2044400550083715317

62407295
>>62388992
Changing your outlook is about changing how you relate to the world, not making the world more comfortable with you. If he's happy (or at least happierk) raging, he should.

62407320
>>62390565
Organize a union.

62407396
Reading this thread has really changed my opinion of the average American. I didn’t know you were all suffering like this. I finally understand Trump voters, I thought they were simply NPCs susceptible to marketing. They’re just stressed to the limit and want to blow the system up no matter what. Makes a bit more sense

I’m going to be nicer to everyone I meet and be more grateful for what I have.

62407517
>>62407396
Nah a lot of it is still NPCs susceptible to marketing. And racism. That said, even as a hardcore progressive, I'll be the first to tell you that Biden made things immeasurably worse, and waterboard me with milk a few times and I'll even admit that Obama didn't do as much for the American people as he could or should have. I genuinely wish he'd been the kind of full-blown socialist globalism clean energy sellout nigger anger monkey he got portrayed as, because then at least we'd have renewable energy and free college to go along with printing and spending trillions of dollars we're never going to get back (we used it on wars and PPP loans asset inflation instead).

I understand the desire to seriously reconsider some fundamental institutions.

62407709
>>62388711
>all women around me have fancy jobs with big salaries
>>62399956

boomers always have the last word on new hires, and they love young woman, I think you can guess why. Boomer niggers will be 85 years old in a wheelchair and still view 18 year olds as their sexual rivals

62407855
>>62388762
This actually the ”i want to attract chad” pose. T. A chad

62408366
>>62407709
This is related to the main reason underqualified men get hired when they do: it's when the boss sees them as a protege. This is why it's useless to apply places where affinity bias isn't working in your favor, and why it's way easier for tall men to get hired. Forget your CV, forget your technical interview; what matters is making the boss feel like they're looking at a younger version of themselves. They want to imbue with their knowledge, so that you can avoid the mistakes they made, and be a sort of redo of their early career.

62408409
>>62407517
Kys commie faggot

62408428
>62408409
A~nd filtered! :)

62408434
>>62388706
The pay is great, you have been reading fake news. And Id rather use my body than get gamer spine sitting around all day like some useless faggot pretending to work all day. Have you even adjusted your calculations for how little respect you earn as a desk faggot? You literally sit there boring your eyes out looking at a screen as your back disintegrates from no use, get fat, your wife leaves you for a tradie who still has vemm and verve while you are a beam counter husk for some globohomo pozzed company thats actively trying to design the tech that will replace you. Have fun.

62408443
>>62408366
>The 70 year old boomers "see themselves in" attractive, retarded young women
DESU the demographic collapse could be solved if young men just did this. Nothing sexier than giving out cushy sinecures.

62408445
>>62407517
Degenerates like you belong in a woodchipper

62408451
>>62408366
Underqualified men get hired because they work for indian tier wages, end of story.

62408561
>>62388676
>PIP ends on Tuesday
My unemployment ends in five weeks so I'm in the same boat as you OP. Except I'm a mongoloid with no degree living in the deep south so maybe I'll manage out in the woods.
>I honestly feel like god did this beacuse its too much bad things happening at once that are culminating into my total demise.
Probably not God causing all this, though a group of people that God has described in the past as rebellious and stiff necked may have something to do with this global cascade of misfortune that is harming both you and the rest of us. I have nothing to give you OP other than don't give up because shit's getting silly and the most interesting/funny worst days may be ahead of us. Try and lay low and live light so you can cruise through the potential coming calamity with peace of mind and a lot of lulz.

62408699
>>62408443
>This is related to the main reason underqualified men get hired when they do
Pls read anon
Though I suppose they do see themselves "in" those women, in way.

item.title: If you’ve held Chainlink for years, ask yourself what you’ve been waiting for. — 152 replies — 179.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
If you’ve held Chainlink for years, ask yourself what you’ve been waiting for.

Open thread • Replies: 152 • Updated: 2026-06-24 22:46 UTC

OP

If you’ve held Chainlink for years, ask yourself what you’ve been waiting for.

LINK is a $5.7B token on $57.5M of annual fees. That’s ~99x revenue, and it has been flat for years, straight through every “banks are coming” cycle. The promise was always that adoption converts into value. It hasn’t. It’s been “soon” since 2020.

So here’s the question no diehard wants to answer:

What fee number, by what date, would make you sell? If LINK still earns ~$57M in 2028, is the thesis wrong, or is it still “soon”? If you can’t name the number and the date, you’re not investing in Chainlink. You’re believing in it. And belief has no exit.

Early replies (first 3)

62382825
never. Its not about the link token, its just about the hope for the future it gives. That is what keeps me going, even if its a delusion. Thus I will never sell, because as long as I hold the token that fantastical delusion can still be real in my mind

62382842
chatgpt post

62382990
>>62382842
OP is a mentally ill faggot

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62400297
>>62400234
>Every post that triggers me is made by the same poster.
Mind breaking heavy bags

62400329
>>62400297
You really have no idea how insanely obvious you are, do you?

62400348
>>62398292
kek
the entire thesis of Chainlink btfo in a single post

62400559
>>62400329
You are deranged lmao, its hilarious how you think you are always talking to 1 person.

62400655
>>62400559
This is a dead board, this thread has been up for almost a week now.
Yet you keep actively seeking out Link threads and then crying about all the "Link shills" you see in them. All the while pretending it's a normal thing to do.

It's not, and you stick out more than ever.

62400679
>dead board
>retarded shill keeps necro bumping his dead shill thread
>hurrrr why does the entire board shit on me?? you're all samefags1!!1!1
stop giving him (You)s and don't forget to sagepost frens

62400691
>>62400679
>shill thread
oof, might wanna check out the OP, samefagboy.

62400897
>>62400655
I just noticed you spamming the thread bieps, just hopped in. Hit you with the reminder of the flippening. You verbally shit yourself again. The regular.

62400962
>>62382813
>white male

no thanks

62401040
>>62400897
>muh flippening
we know you're a xerpie, yes

62401056
>>62400962
Russians arent white

62401136
>>62401056
niggers aren't black

62401340
>still going at it for 5 days
removing IP count was a mistake

62401653
Rome wasn't built in a day.

62401756
Didn't read never selling. Simple as.

62401863
>>62382825
This and the 4.32% roi for doing absolutely nothing

>>62383155
Tbh the only reason literal nobodies as us had a chance to stake was because we held in private wallet a during the 50$ peak, and thus could opt in as I met the onchain criteria, so due to that im happy in the end

62402855
ok, sold the rest, can't stand these anons making excuses anymore. have fun

62402860
>>62402855
Me too!
I'll post my sell order as evidence in a minute

62402872
Ada’s death spiral is glorious. At least they can all sell now and move over to xrp because how fucking stupid they are.

62403508
Imagine "investing" in OpenAI credits to try and make a profit just because they're gonna release a new model.

That's how silly buying link tokens in order to make money from new releases sounds

62403613
shut the actual fuck up and line up for the vaccine incels

62403671
>>62395706
Oh I remember this deranged pseud, he's the one who proudly claimed that he invested in the kike grifter shitcoin xrp because it represents his "Christian values" and anyone who attacks xrp is basically one of the "pharisees"

It was hard to believe that someone could be that retarded but biz is a wasteland now, and he's one of the more retarded mutants apparently

62403684
>>62403613
I am unvaxxed and I hold link

62403723
Sergey?

62403906
>>62394749
Imagine defending a guy named fucking Gensler

62404804
>>62398292
It depends on the location of the employees. How hard does McDonalds work to obsfucate the price differences between different locations?

62404810
>>62403723
No think it was some fortune top 10 guy

62405991
Money stolen by a literal fucking pajeet
People need to start doing something about these scammers. Do they plan on hosting another smart con or do they dare not face link holders?
When they stop doing smart con you know that they're finally pulling the rug

62405996
>>62405991
The pajeet fucker in question

62408458
>>62403508
we're about to make stupid amounts of money and you're yapping about nonsense. you're most definitely going to blow all of your gains on dumb shit and return to being a poorcel.

item.title: MSTR and STRC and literally in a Luna tier collapse and nobody is panicking? — 39 replies — 156.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
MSTR and STRC and literally in a Luna tier collapse and nobody is panicking?

Open thread • Replies: 39 • Updated: 2026-06-24 23:24 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62405530
why would i panic when i'm not holding any crapto?

62405594
I hold the real deal not the proxy.

62405608
My fiat bags are ready to slurp the $20k bottom in October

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62406600
>>62406041
I think they'll do what you're saying. Maybe sell off between $5-10B worth in one go to secure dividend funding and announce a lock-up on the remaining Bitcoin with a set number of years to try to reduce panic selling once it becomes public

62406601
>>62406041
>>62406565
Saylor is a retard and his ponzi is holding crypto back. Yes likely he is fine and it will collapse only 2029 but this is so retarded

62406637
>>62406363
10x worse, Luna didn't cause a massive selloff of BTC. The problem with impacting the main asset of a whole market is that it will spread to the market as whole. Luna already showed this in a smaller proportion. Expect many protocols to depeg and break.

62406662
>>62406601
Holding crypto back from what?

62406670
>>62406662
From number going up

62406739
>>62406565
He might as well dillute his shares to a single digit, what will happen is literally a death spiral, the market will stay away from MSTR and creditors might as well stop lending him money at low interest due to either a drop in his company rating and general macroeconomic data (rate hikes).

Also, the problem with STRC depegging and not being able to get back to $100 is that the interest he will be paying will start to exponentially rise. The basis was 11.5% apy, right now it's sitting at 14%. Those 3.5% is a big difference in his balance sheet, if STRC reaches $60, the interest is going to reach 19%, $50 is 23%. So you see where this leads to? If at some point it get worringly at $20, you are talking about 57.5% of annual, semi-monthly dividends to distribute. If he has cash for 2 years as of today, that means he would have left only a fourth of that amount, so 6 months AND then the obligations from his debts.

Anything that yields a really high disproportionate return relative to the whole market is really looked frowned upon by the market and investors, this is literally a ponzi and a death spiral. This could ultimately lead him to sell a portion of BTC still this year, and that could impact the market pretty badly, for instance, just see how the market reacts to any news of Bitcoin from MtGox that is retrieved and sent to exchanges., and that's a fraction of what Saylor has.

62406788
>>62406739
Typical bogus assumptions, like nearly every anti-Strategy post.
The STR products are very defensive. The dividend is voluntary. They can and will defend it, failing to do so would be a PR nightmare, but they are not legally obligated to run their entire company and the entire BTC market into the ground to defend it.
If it falls to $20, they can buy back that debt at a 80% discount and retire the dividend. That's a great deal.
Per the official prospectus of this product they also have an option to buy it back at par ($100), so there is no requirement to pay absurdly high dividends eternally if it turns out that this isn't working.
Outside of this option they can also start buybacks below par to retire that debt at a discount.
The basic sanity check you need is this:
BTC holdings: 50b
Debt (STR* + convertible): 22b

The convertible debt will take 3 years and is only 6.7b. The STR debt is designed to be spaced out over many more years, and they can suspend it as they see fit.

If BTC gets cut in half or worse and goes into a 10 year bear market, this thing isn't going to work out, but in that case who even cares for any treasury company anyway? We will have moved on to other things.

62406800
>>62405528
I lost all my money longing the way down already

62406801
>>62406565
that's good to know but dividends are optional. If things are really bad they can just not pay them rather than dump a lot of btc.

The fact that they have enough cash for 1 year of dividend is good.

>>62406637
Luna was worse. it was a stable coin pegged to a shitcoin. This isn't really the same thing. People can dump all their mstr and it goes to 0$ and btc will keep its value. The opposite isn't true however. Unless i'm missing something?

>>62406739
Wait, so strc is actually pegged to 100$ value$? Yeah ok then maybe you have a point. all of this confusing, i looked it up and it says STRC is a preferrential share? these holders come first for dividends.
You're fudding me out of buying mstr below 50$ as i planned

62406840
>>62405528
gonna buy strc at $10 and make 115% a year

62406848
>>62406788
> bogus
Stop shitting the board with your claude slop posts. Fag.

62406888
>>62406637
>We're now in the "total cataclysm imminent" phase of the bear market

Holy shit I'm buying

62406891
>>62406801
He's bullshitting. If the price of STRC is $100 they're paying $11.50/share/year. If the price is $50 they're still paying $11.50/share/year. Unless they crank up dividends to try to maintain $100/share, but they're not obligated to, and it makes way more sense to just buyback shares at a discount, retire that obligation, wait for Bitcoin to drag itself along, and then start selling STRC at $100 again.

62406897
>>62406848
Claude is amazing, thank you for the compliment. My posts are completely handwritten. English isn't even my native language. I guess the coolest high schoolers nowadays don't say "bogus" and will remember this in the future.
I only clarified the meaning of the "Earliest Call Date" on https://www.strategy.com/credit using AI (not Claude).
>>62406788
>Wait, so strc is actually pegged to 100$ value$?
STRC is not pegged. It has a $100 target price controlled by the dividend. There's a set of rules to respond to prices too far below $100 by raising the dividend incrementally, thereby hopefully raising it to par eventually.
What naysayers also miss is that every $100 share was sold for the promise to pay $11.5 per year. If you want your $100 back, you have to sell into the open market. This is why the BTC bought using STR gives them a long runway.
As stated above, if the whole thing doesn't work out, they have the right to buy all of it back at $100.

62406933
>>62405528
saylorcoin is fucked till hes out

62406934
>>62406788
You got a point. He can rebuy but the dividends will accrue with interest, he will need to pay someday.

His bitcoin holdings are 50b now, at the money, we are expecting bitcoin to drop at least 30% further and hit 40k, so that will get closer and closer to his debt. From experience and seeing what normies are saying, they are also claiming 40k so I expect going even lower.

>>62406801

I don't want to fud I want to buy MSTR to have an extra exposure, it's interesting to buy at really low prices. He might not go bankrupt, but I would pay attention to his company until this bear ends.

62406939
>>62406801
See >>62406897 (wrongly replied to myself).
Also, the real risks are that dividend payments from STR products can be suspended as they see fit, and there is no requirement to protect the principal at any cost.
This is why the death spiral fantasies of Strategy running itself and BTC into the ground are unrealistic. They don't have to do this, so they won't.
However, rugpulling STR products, which they could do in theory, would hurt their credit line, stock price, etc. If you check the STRC holders and say Yahoo Finance you'll see that BlackRock, VanEck and Fidelity ETFs put hundreds of millions into this. Strategy is unlikely to rugpull them.
Realistically they can easily sit out a bear market for multiple years, they'll easily be able to pay the dividends and make some effort to get back to $100 par, but you can't depend on this especially in the near term.

62406959
Where Saylor get the money? There must be some glitch.. he keep buying at loss and pay High interest but he don't produce nothing useful. That's like a life cheat code, the ultimate Ponzi materialization

62406974
>>62406959
he just needs to increase the strc yield to 35%

62407037
>>62406934
>His bitcoin holdings are 50b now, at the money, we are expecting bitcoin to drop at least 30% further and hit 40k, so that will get closer and closer to his debt. From experience and seeing what normies are saying, they are also claiming 40k so I expect going even lower.
The price of STRC is controlled using the open market.
Strategy sold each STRC for $100 and bought $100 worth of BTC at (I think) an average of something like $75k.
Strategy committed to paying $11.5 per share per year (likely going up a bit in the future to try and restore the par value).
If they are able to sell the BTC they purchased using the $100 at their purchase price, they have 9 years of dividend coverage just from selling the BTC bought using STRC.
If the BTC they purchased falls 50% when they sell, they still have 4.5 years of dividend coverage from selling that BTC alone - ignoring all of their other holdings, ignoring common stock dilution, and ignoring other credit market options.
Notably, Strategy is not required to buy back any shares and give you back the $100 directly. If you want your $100 back, you have to sell into the open market. At this time only dividend, which they did commit to paying (but which they can suspend at any time) is used to try and keep the shares at $100. I think in the future they are likely to buy back to stabilize the price, but there is no requirement to death-spiral into anything.

62407111
>>62406974
Looks like a fraud to me

62407163
Bitcoin is getting Stretched!

62407588
I have a feeling MSTR is in the same situation that FTX was. Their books are all good, they have strong investments, but they have just enough weird shit going on that they can't survive a bank run in the middle of a bear market.

62407749
>>62406959
Yes. Thats the problem. This retard thought everyone would switch to Bitcoin so it would be fine but it doesnt look like it.

62407785
>>62407588
>Their books are all good
FTX's books weren't all good. They had a giant hole because the weasel thought stoplosses were for pussies, so Alameda lost billions, and SBF funneled customers money from FTX to Alameda to try to recover the losses

62407826
>>62407785
what i mean is, FTX would have been fine/profitable in a normal bull or bear market. Their mistake was having enough risk that they were unable to survive a bear market + black swan

62407842
>>62407826
nah they were misusing customer deposits. The slightest hint of withdrawal issues would have and did cause them to collapse

62407860
>>62407826
>they would have been fine if the fraud hadn't come to light
Yeah, I mean... So would Bernie Madoff

62407942
>>62405528
>and literally in a Luna
hello saar

62408624
>>62407826
strategy is orders of magnitude better at risk management than FTX. Look at their debt ratio

>NEET
>rentoid
>not enough to buy a house without having to work again

>but, can't do nothing obviously

I have some business ventures starting up that make a little money, and should make more in a few years.

just wondering is anyone else in a similar situation?

62407886

>>62407772

62408633

62408643
>>62408593

62408655

item.title: Polkadot update — 32 replies — 49.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot update

Open thread • Replies: 32 • Updated: 2026-06-24 22:58 UTC

OP

ITS HERE

XRP, ETHEREUM AND LINK IS COOKED

https://medium.com/polkadot-network/dot-dao-and-the-need-for-jamkb-a069e72e9728

https://news.openshore.io/web3-summit-2026-recap.html

Early replies (first 3)

62399118
5. Privacy is a Competitive Advantage
In a world of surveillance capitalism, Polkadot's emphasis on privacy-by-default and user agency differentiates it sharply from other ecosystems. The proof-of-personhood protocol and private payment demonstrations showed concrete technical approaches, not just rhetoric.

Do they think big Google and Facebook will let them get away with this?

62399184
After further research

It seems we have a fork of consensus

https://forum.polkadot.network/t/introducing-a-new-jam-token/13029

https://medium.com/polkadot-network/dot-dao-and-the-need-for-jamkb-a069e72e9728

So what the fuck is JAM and how will it bring value back to DOT marketcap?

62399190
>The JAM protocol presupposes the existence of a token to ensure soundness in its state usage. I argue that DOT would make a highly suboptimal choice and that a new, fixed-issuance token to represent the new Polkadot resource of JAM state makes the most sense. Assuming DOT DAO were to launch a Polkadot JAM for its upgrade, then this new resource-access token would be wholly owned by DOT DAO by default. DOT utility would continue as at present, implying control of DOT DAO — and with it, effective ownership of the Polkadot JAM — as well as the means for staking, collateral and coretime payment within the Polkadot ecosystem.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62399197
>>62399190
oh boy, another scam

62399251
Slurped

62399395
>>62399197

Linkie crying seeing another crypto vibing

62400350
>>62399184
a new resource is being introduced to Polkadot with the JAM upgrade (fast shared storage like RAM) and you need one JAMKB per KB of RAM you want to use on the network, so you can't buy more tokens than there are KBs of RAM

62400419
>>62399084
DOT will make us all rich beyond our Wildest dreams!!

62401678
>>62399084

62401689
>>62401678
they had a grapheneOS build for the event so it is likely that will happen

62401700

62401739

Just bought a few k
We'll see how this goes

62401877

62401946

62402307

62402317

62402326

62402419

62403350

62403448

it wont stay under a dollar for long

item.title: BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!!! — 22 replies — 32.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!!!

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-06-24 22:56 UTC

OP

>Today, Chainlink, FairSquareLab, a first-mover in core infrastructure within the Korean digital asset ecosystem, UniKA (Unified Korea Alliance), which represents the Korean coalition behind Project Pangea and is comprised of a steering committee of 5 entities (Shinhan Bank, JB Bank, Kbank, FairSquareLab, and OBDIA) along with 10+ participating Korean commercial banks, and Qivalis, a euro stablecoin consortium powered by 37 leading European banks, announced Project Pangea, a strategic, collaborative task force focused on redefining global foreign exchange markets by unlocking real-time, stablecoin-based cross-border settlement models.


https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/chainlink-and-multinational-banking-consortia-launch-project-pangea-to-develop-t0-settlement-framework-for-international-fx-markets-302807910.html

item.title: Day 342 of snailposting every day until LINK is at $100 — 6 replies — 24.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Day 342 of snailposting every day until LINK is at $100

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-06-24 23:24 UTC

OP

Day 342 of snailposting every day until LINK is at $100

Early replies (first 3)

62405634
>>62405471
based

62406642
>>62405471
Imagine being OP

62407027
>>62405471
>>62405634
>>62406642
It's obvious I fucked up pretty badly holding my LINK this long but I feel like my only option really now is to just wait and see if it ever does the massive pump we're all expecting.

The cognitive dissonance of watching adoption after adoption happen and all the big tradfi stuff play out while watching the token price plummet is maddening. Either at some point there will legit be a "singularity" where within a very short time span the token price goes to hundreds of dollars+ per, or it's just destined to be a non speculative asset forever. The former seems way more likely to happen, but not having any clue of WHEN that would happen is rough. 9 fucking years of my life.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62407846
>>62407027
You are fucking retarded. What is the revenue of Chainlink? They are just burning money and guess where is the funding coming from? Apart from this, the tokenomics is just stupidly useless, the LINK token exists and is demanded by payment abstraction but it is not necessary; at any moment they can just pivot.

>Adoption after adoption
It doesnt fucking matter. I repeat, not because you have adoption, means higher price. Chainlink is NOT a good business right now, it does not generate profits vs all the money they are burning right now.

>but they will connect the entire financial market
The market does not give a fuck. And unfortunately, what you have to understand is that price is driven by demand, and if no one gives a fuck about it, no matter how good it is, it will suck in price, and more specially on something that is driven by speculation. Boring unknown but good companies that no one gives a fuck still go up eventually and slowly because they are “sound investment”, they are backed by a business generating profits; your little stupid digital link coin is not.

>But just wait… you will see
Its been more than 6 years and still no profits. The big adoptions are already priced in. There are too many government blockers.

Your only hope is to wait for a general crypto bullrun, which is cringe because you are going to think its because of chainlink but its actually just crypto sentiment. Just watch the correlation of link and alt market price, it has been the same since 2021, its a dead general crypto coin that just follows whatever alt is doing.

62407870
>>62407027
that's right, the longer you hold, the more likely it is that is will go up. just imagine the likelihood at 20 years in, at that point, it has to happen in like two weeks tops

62408629
keep crawling lil buddy

62407950
>>62406449
HOLY SHIT! MY SIDES! AAAAAHHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHHAHAHAHHAHAHAHAHAH LOL LMAO EVEN AHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHA BAGGIES ON SUICIDE WATCH! LOOOOL ROFL! MUH UTILITY! MUH FUTURE OF FUCKING FINANCE!

62407956
>>62407950
oops wrong thread

62407963
>>62406474

This. You say magic jewish number go down. But I still have the same number of ounces as yesterday.

62407970
>>62406474
that's called "bagholding"

62408007
I hold silver but I'm glad it's dropping, it's not an investment it's protection against the economy imploding, the cheaper it is the more I can buy

62408050
>>62408007
>I'm glad it's dropping
>so I can buy more
Lmao. Precious metals really are just Funko Pops for shutin schizophrenic virgin retards.

62408105
>>62406474
Based

>>62406449
Anyone whose held this for a good while will have patience and nerves like ice water. I never see BTC maxies talk about stacking Sats any more, perhaps because they know fundamentally its different. Zoom out, on the globe not the chart, the world is on fire and getting worse. Everything is getting hit, I posted here for weeks that the gold sell off was front running the stock market and bonds. Countries are going to be selling everything to stay alive, but Gold remains the most universally liquid without causing tge US to sperg. I'll say it again, where precious metals go you're going with it whether its crypto or NVDA, there is only a commodities bull left to invest in and even that will get hit. Cash chads stay winning but spend that money wisely at the everything sale; the US stock market had been too lucky and cancer has been allowed to grow. Normally recessions purge such things but its like when dead wood piles up due to a lack of forest fires. Also, food prices aren't going to be fully realized until october.

62408330
>>62406449
>>62406627
>>62406603
Final boss' desperation special. Something is coming. They wouldn't draw attention to themselves if it weren't serious.

62408343
>>62408050

what you dont understand here? its not a quick gamble...
so called "bagholding" was the plan all along!!

62408348
>>62408105
Just in time for me to start my bulk :')

62408419
>>62407950
kek cryptards are getting brain damage from being fucked so hard

62408483
>>62406449
i sold at the top

item.title: Binance out of the EU? — 34 replies — 18.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Binance out of the EU?

Open thread • Replies: 34 • Updated: 2026-06-24 21:45 UTC

OP

How is this going to affect the crypto market?

Early replies (first 3)

62404563
>>62404556
>the crypto market
what?

you thought theres still a market?

lol

why you think every pump gets sold off?

underwater MMs are slowly offloading to $0 into ever-decreasing demand thats your market

62404564
>>62404556
why are you still talking about crypto even? Everyone with a brain has moved onto stocks

62404566
>>62404563
MMs? Market makers? Dont those cunts make their money out of the spread?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62404608
>>62404556
peepoo ll hold their own coins and slimy cz wont be able to manipulate the prices so easily.

62404611
>>62404556
see how dast the lil droids sliding the thred, it fears :D

62404615
>>62404556
Anyone here still using binance?

62404622
>>62404556
What kind of idiot uses Binance?

62404679
>>62404622
It seems that there are a lot of idiots anon, is the biggest exchange in the world.

62404688
>>62404622
Whats the better alternative? And please dont tell mot robinhood or some shit

62404733
>>62404556
That could cause a dump

62404795
>>62404679
They spoof volume, if they had the same regs they'd be smaller than coinbase

62404802
>>62404795
They operate in europe so they have the same regulations than coinbase, they are the biggest one because they operate on the most regions

62404814
>>62404802
This thread is about them not operating in Europe anymore because they dont meet the regs

62404825
>>62404814
The things is that this could cause a massive withdrawal, do they really have your coins in there?

62404832
>>62404814
Well they still are, no official statement was issued to users yet

62404835
>>62404825
Im not too worried about it, its been very telegraphed and binance has handled stuff like this before without much issue

Its much more lucrative for them to keep collecting fees rather than rug

62404874
>>62404688
Dexes. Only two limitations come to my mind: 1) can be hard/expensive to trade fiat->crypto and 2) you can't get "physical delivery" of dumb chain coins like BTC or LTC that don't have trustless bridges to smart chains. For everything else dexes are just better.

62405781
>>62404556
it's only temporary as long as they don't have their licence

62405795
>>62404874
dexes suck, only order books matter for trading and the volume is low, exchanges have more funds to get, that's without accounting the fact that they rely on crypto which is not needed, crypto is not replacing centralized servers

62405831
>>62404556
Europeans are poor, hence europoor. They barely have money to invest. Sometimes I think I'm blessed that my German Borderline ex-wife divorced me, imagine living in a country where you barely can make ends meet AND life alongside jeets, sandniggers and kikes that sabotage a whole continent.

62405835
>>62405831
Just so sum up:
>How is this going to affect the crypto market?
It won't.

62405842
CZ will finally use his Samson Option.
October 10th was just the appetizer.

62406979
>>62405795
>only order books matter
You are only thinking about AMMs. Every perp dex is order book.
>the volume is low
Hyperliquid has literally higher volume than Coinbase.
>exchanges have more funds to get
No clue what you meant by this.
>they rely on crypto which is not needed
One is leaving EU because of regulation while the other stays accessible globally without KYC. You tell me which one is replacing which.

62407175
>>62405831
Isn't texas overrun with jeets and scamming africans as we speak? Lol

62407374
>>62404832
I received this mail today 3 hours ago, I have no crypto since years but still have that account.

62407444
>>62407374

62407464
>>62407374
Interesting, i also have a european based account and did not receive any such email yet, it just said in the app that they are now regulated under some abu Dhabi regulation agency

62407472
>>62407444
Funds are safu lol (I have no funds I don't care)

62407521
>>62407444
So no margin trading whatsoever for all eurocucks from next month onwards? Oh this is gonna be fine lol.

62407587
>>62407521
Actual BCE leader Christine Lagarde hates crypto, I'm not mad about binance they are probably a big money laundering operation . They are launching digital euro soon that works better then crypto

62407893
>>62406979
hyperliquid have 10x less volume than coinbase and order book dexes are a minority
derivatives don't matter, only spot matter for making real profits and moving markets

62408104
>>62404688
I went with Kraken

62408164
>>62407587
>works better than crypto

absolutely cattle

item.title: VOO and Chill — 58 replies — 13.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
VOO and Chill

Open thread • Replies: 58 • Updated: 2026-06-24 19:27 UTC

OP

The more I look into investing and compare the historical returns of certain ETFs / individual stocks it seems like the whole philosophy of “VOO and Chill” is equal parts really smart and retarded
It’s like a no brainer for money but even now as long as the market is in a growth period shouldn’t you at least do QQQ?
It seems so simple that’d you miss a lot of upside but the returns are honestly not bad and extremely consistent
How accurate is this assessment? It’s both so simple and guaranteed that everyone should it but also too safe so you end up missing out on more upside
I’m talking 5-20 years investing here not trading

Early replies (first 3)

62400515
Boomer funds are gay and lame

62400554
>>62400515
What qualifies as one
Why
What crypto do you want to shill

62400560
boglehead method is the tried and tested way, buy the market with an index fund that has a low fee, invest monthly, do it consistently for decades and you WILL be rich
but biz/anons don't care about being rich when they're older, they want to be rich NOW
greed is a deadly sin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62403053
>>62403044
The median 64 year old american has 400k to retire on and they seem to be ok with it.
That's what middle class means in america. Of course 1 in 10 Americans have a net worth of 1 million, but that's not controlled for age.

62403140
>>62400636
Im the second one.
Yeehaw

62403184
>>62403140
same

62403215
>>62403030
>The average person is pretty poor.

Kind of.

>The U.S. median household income is $83,730. For individual personal income, the median figure is $45,140. -- St. Louis FED

Most Americans can afford to save more than $100/month, especially if they are married or have a SO, but most Americans really don't think about the future, so they finance the best lifestyle the bank is willing to finance instead of living well beneath their means. Most Americans should not be going to Disneyworld or on vacations every year, especially on credit, but they do and this leads them to get into debt, making it "impossible" to save money.

I try to save as much as possible because no way am I not going to settle for $200k in the year of our Lord 2060. That's broke money today, much less in 40 years when the USD is further in the toilet.

62404485
>>62400560
I want to be free. The colour of my bugatti is meat colored

62404617
>>62400493
it's over

62404649
>>62400515
And jewish

62404732
>>62400493
Im all in on aussie etf covering nasdaq NDQ, 60k dollars so far. But will probably start getting other stuff to be safe. I figured its fine if it goes down 50% at this early stage cause 30k isnt world ending

62404755
>>62400493
forget VOO and forget QQQ. AVUV and AVDV is where you should be, mix in some VTI and VEU so you don't kill yourself. Whatever that threshold is for you.

62404820
>>62404755
look at this shit, Small cap value beats its market by 3% consistently over long periods, doesn't matter if its US or international. QQQ outperforming in the last 10 years is a tiny sample and not significant. Don't trust it, its a mirage. You're a boglehead trust the statistics, numbers don't lie.

62404829
>>62404820
Yeah QQQ is simply doing good due to a highly concentrated growth period
This is interesting though is there any catch or downside to discuss with small cap? Why exactly is it consistently better?
I got $55000 in VOO and some in SCHD…….Man this is making me realize when I first started I put $5000 into SCHD before realizing chasing dividends at age 26 is pointless

62404895
>>62404829
the catch is that its not very correlated with the broader market and its more volatile (more risk means more return, and in this case its actually true. Most of the time when you see people in /smg/ talk about more risk its not actually coming with more quantifiable return. More risk doesn't magically make more return), so if you are 100% SCV there may be stretches where you have to watch VOO have solid returns while you get nothing and kill yourself, but the periods when SCV beats the market it kills.

As for why it beats the market. You will have to read farma and french's research papers, the short of it is that there are quantifiable metrics which can explain stock outperformance, they are called 5 factors and they are Momentum, Size, Value, Profitability and Volatility. Small cap value stocks overlap positively with the most factors.

62404949
>>62404755
I just opened a new stock account strictly for etf investing.
I’m thinking of doing 2 shares of AVUV and 2 shares of AVDV each month indefinitely. (1 share each week alternating between the two).
Wish me luck.

62405063
The S&P 500 is more crash proof now just because so many Boomers rely on it for their 401ks and Social Security is only 5 years away from being completely depleted. If we went through a lost decade like the 2000s there would be Great Depression levels of disparity as the biggest consumer class would be gone. Given how they responded to the 2008 crisis and Covid the powers will keep stocks propped up no matter the cost

62405112
>>62400493
wait until this current bear routing is over. Right now you get nothing but declines for the foreseeable future. But that will bottom out then it can resume and your philosophy works

62405201
>>62400554
notice how whenever you call out a boomer fund or jewish 401k, they sperg out and start insulting you and calling you stupid, gee great way to sell your "product"

62405226
>>62405112
Yeah, I got time and will (hopefully) only be making more money for when prices drop too
Don’t wanna retire at 65 but wouldn’t mind 40/45 if I can hit 150-200k invested soon

62405252
>>62405201
Are you replying to the wrong post all that guy did was ask questions

62405400
>>62404617
they predicted a 5-6% YTD gain for 2026 and we're already above that, lying shits.

62406764
>>62400493
Rather buy VT. Not so bullish on USA anymore

62406776
>>62406764
Still, conceptually it’s sound right?

62406804
>>62400493
>QQQ
if you want more returns (with more risk), you should do something like leverage + vt (or similar) instead of voo (you should at least do vti) and global small cap value, not sector etfs

62406811
>>62406804
in English doc

62406843
>>62406811
Alright! I'm gonna explain it like you're 5 years old!

Think of investing like choosing a basket of seeds: VOO is like only planting the biggest U.S. trees, VTI is like planting the whole U.S. garden, VT is like planting the whole world garden, and global small cap value is like adding lots of tiny, scrappy plants that have sometimes grown faster over long periods; if you want more possible growth and can stomach bigger ups and downs, using leverage is like turning up the volume on a diversified basket, which can help more than just buying one loud sector ETF, because sectors are a bet on one part of the economy and can stay out of favor for a long time, while broad diversification plus leverage is usually a cleaner way to aim for higher returns without relying on one slice of the market.

62406996
>>62406804
I am a noob and leverage seems scary desu like I will owe that money eventually right

62407040
>>62406996
yes, I'm not into leverage either, it evolves its own risks, but if you are not risk averse, it's better than gambling/prediction markts/crypto etc.

62407059
>>62400493
Voo used to pay more dividends before it was all fag7. Its basically the same as the nasdaq for the past decade

62407078
>>62400493
yeah I have like almost half a million in VTI right now, and I just need to sit on it for another 10 years. Its funny, I get excited whenever VTI goes down because it means i get a little bonus next deposit

62407086
Man I want to just have more principal to invest rather than chasing returns
Anyone know of good side hustles or AI grifts anyone can do
Idc as long as it’s triple digits a month at least

62407097
>>62406776
with Trump's economy + trade wars, VXUS actually went up a ton during the period and now a lot of countries are getting less and less dependent on America. If/when the AI bubble ever pops then you're going to be a lot more insulated since the American economy is being propped up by it.
